paragraph: Single-family homes in Ridgefield are moving at a median of $745,000 with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.0 and roughly 38 percent of homes selling above asking. The median days on market sits at 26, which puts this in competitive but balanced territory. Sellers aren't dictating terms here, but priced homes are moving steadily. Multi-family properties tell a different story. Nine sales over seven months landed at a median of $999,999, and 56 percent sold above asking with a median of 16 days on market. That's genuinely competitive, with multiple offer situations showing up regularly in this tier. The sale-to-list ratio at 1.004 confirms it. Condos and townhouses are the lightest category with only five sales at a median of $275,000, all hitting list price exactly. The median days on market of 30 suggests these need more active marketing to draw interest, though the small sample makes it hard to call the trend definitive. If you're weighing single-family versus multi-family in Ridgefield, the single-family market gives you more breathing room to negotiate. Multi-family properties move faster and command premiums, which means if you're selling at that tier you'll likely see competition, but if you're buying you'll need to move decisively.
